en-US.Heading: # Data Engineering Projects!
en-US.Description: In here I have a couple projects fucusing on Data Engineering problems, such as data ingestion, ELT/ETL Pipelines and Data Quality Checks.

Projects.Name: Brapi
Projects.ProjectDescription:
This project was aimed at keeping a medallion architecture using databricks, while pulling stock ticker prices from Brapi's API.
 Data flows from the API calls into bronze layer, in silver we perform data schema validations and other data quality validations. 
        The project is available at: https://github.com/henriqueetges/MyDatalake

Contents.Name: Ingestion
Contents.Description:
Ingestion was done through the BRAPI API. Because of free tier constraints, I had to create script to fetch the list of available tickers and later process each ticker individually in order to get historical prices. 
 Free tier constraints also meant that the script could only run twice a month, which proved to be a challenge to data integrity.  The results of the request were saved in databricks DBFS and processed in batches of 50 endpoint calls.

 ![Model](media/ingestion.jpeg)

Contents.Name: ELT
Contents.Description:
After reaching the bronze layer, the resulting data was split in two datasets (Prices and Summary Profile). 
 - Prices 
 Prices was processed out of a struct column containing historical prices. Even through the main endpoint returned daily prices, because of free tier limitations I could only run the requests twice a month. 
 - Summary Profile
 Contained data about the company, such as number of executives, adresses and etc. This data was mostly complete, but it had some data integrity issues. So I decided to run data integrity validations in this project

Contents.Name: Data Integrity
Contents.Description: Because of data integrity issues with some datasets,  I have decided to explore data quality metrics. I had started testing using popular packages such as GreatExpectations, but didn't really like how it came out, specially because I wanted to store the results of the data quality checks in a certain way. For that, I created the project https://github.com/henriqueetges/MyExpectations, which intends to use a metada approach to perform data quality validations. I used *pyspark* to the testing logic.

Contents.Name: DLT
Contents.Description:
his is another piece of my Datalake implementation, where I am slowly transitioning the ELT/ETL scripts from normal batch operations 
 into DLT pipelines in Databricks.
 You can find some examples in my DataLake repository, here: https://github.com/henriqueetges/MyDatalake
